title: iCLUSTA+ vs XServer for WordPress
description: iCLUSTA+ vs XServer for WordPress. Review official pricing, SLA, support, CMS, and contract terms.
lead: iCLUSTA+ is broader business hosting for websites, email, WordPress, and Movable Type. XServer for WordPress is more focused on managed WordPress operations.
author: Luqevora.com Editorial Team

sections.heading: Bottom line
body: iCLUSTA+ is broader business hosting for websites, email, WordPress, and Movable Type. XServer for WordPress is more focused on managed WordPress operations.

sections.heading: Platform scope
body: The choice depends on whether email and multiple CMS products must share one contract.

sections.heading: Availability and support
body: Compare SLA scope, support expertise, migration coverage, and recovery terms.

sections.heading: CMS operations
body: Test updates, staging, backups, user administration, and mail delivery before committing.

sections.heading: Cost checks
body: Compare the full commitment including surcharges, domains, and paid assistance.

sections.heading: Different operating models
body: iCLUSTA+ is a general business hosting platform that combines websites, mail, databases, WordPress, and Movable Type. XServer for WordPress is positioned around managed WordPress operations, including specialist assistance and WordPress-focused maintenance.
Organizations that require corporate mail and more than one CMS may value the broader iCLUSTA+ scope. Teams standardizing entirely on WordPress may value the narrower managed model.

sections.heading: Administration and updates
body: Test plugin updates, theme changes, user permissions, staging, backup restoration, malware response, and DNS changes. Record which tasks the provider performs, which tasks remain with the customer, and which require a paid request.
A managed label does not remove the need for governance. The customer still needs owners for content approval, administrator accounts, third-party plugins, and business continuity.

sections.heading: Cost and migration
body: Compare the complete contract, including surcharge, domain, SSL, mail, migration, restore, and specialist support. A service that includes more operational work can be economical even when its base fee is higher.
Before switching, migrate a non-production copy and verify forms, scheduled tasks, redirects, authentication, mail delivery, and rollback procedures.

sections.heading: Procurement checklist
body: Before approval, obtain a written quotation or save the checkout summary. Record the plan, contract term, surcharge, domain registration and renewal, certificate options, migration assistance, restore fees, and cancellation procedure. Assign an owner for DNS, mail, CMS updates, backups, and incident communication.

Run a non-production test using the expected number of domains, mailboxes, databases, scheduled jobs, and administrator accounts. Measure migration time, confirm outbound and inbound mail, test a backup restoration, and document the rollback path before changing production DNS.

sections.heading: Security and governance
body: Provider controls do not replace customer governance. Use named administrator accounts, multifactor authentication where available, least-privilege access, current CMS components, external backups, and a documented offboarding process. Review logs and provider notices regularly.

For regulated or sensitive workloads, confirm data location, subprocessors, retention, access records, vulnerability handling, and contractual responsibilities with the provider. A shared hosting plan may be suitable for a public website but not for every application or data classification.

sections.heading: Ongoing review
body: Recheck pricing and specifications at renewal because surcharges, capacity, supported software, and campaign terms can change. Compare the actual annual invoice and support usage with the original estimate.
Track uptime, page performance, mail delivery, restore tests, support response, and administrator effort. These operating measurements provide a better renewal decision than marketing claims alone.

faqs.question: Is iCLUSTA+ intended for business use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Its positioning, SLA, mail, SSL, and support options target organizational website operations.

faqs.question: Should buyers compare only the monthly fee?
faqs.answer: No. Include surcharges, domains, options, migration, recovery, and commitment terms.
